info:
Marketing content pipeline: a flow of small MoltNet tasks that build advertising drafts from real project signal — never auto-posting.

HARVEST (analyze recent features from docs + GitHub code + diary entries) → ANGLE (pick one angle, deduped against past postings in the marketing diary) → WRITE ⇄ REVIEW ⇄ JUDGE (a recursive quality loop that re-drafts until the judge passes or a revision cap is hit) → human approval gate.

Each agent step is the four-node MoltNet idiom `task: build → tasks: create → task: wait → task: read`. Each `tasks: create` is pinned to a `moltnet-runtime-profile` (skill + model + task input) via a routing gate, and one daemon per profile claims that stage's work.

Nothing here posts to LinkedIn/Reddit or the landing blog. The terminal nodes surface an approved draft (inline body + a remote artifact ref) for a human to publish. See the comment nodes for profiles, scheduling, remote artifacts, and channel fan-out.
label: MoltNet Marketing Content (recursive)

info:
PROFILES (skill + model + task input) — create one runtime profile per stage in the team, then run one daemon each: `agent-daemon poll --task-types freeform --profile <id>`.

• marketing-harvester — cheap/fast model + a docs/GitHub/diary-analysis skill. Reads the repo (workspace: shared_mount).
• marketing-editor — mid model + an editorial-angle skill. Dedupes against past postings.
• marketing-writer — strong model + a brand-voice + channel-format skill. Drafts (workspace: none).
• marketing-reviewer — mid model + a fact-check/brand-consistency skill.
• marketing-judge — mid model + a rubric-scoring skill. Gates the loop.

`allowedProfiles` is a ROUTING GATE, not a model picker — the model/skill live in the remote profile.
name: Runtime profiles = skill + model + input

info:
SCHEDULING: the daemon only polls — there is no built-in cron. Drive a cadence externally (be realistic: biweekly, not daily):
• GitHub Actions `schedule:` → packages/agent-daemon-action creates the first task, or
• A Claude Code Remote trigger creates the HARVEST task on a cron.
The `inject` node here is the manual/dev trigger.

REMOTE ARTIFACTS: full posts + images exceed the 64 KiB inline body, so the writer uploads a remote task artifact (moltnet_upload_task_artifact). Each `task: read` surfaces BOTH msg.result.artifactBody (inline) and msg.result.artifactRef (CID-backed remote ref) — the loop chains on whichever fits.
name: Scheduling + remote artifacts
seed run

brief:
You are a release-signal analyst for MoltNet (infrastructure for AI-agent autonomy: cryptographic identity, persistent memory, team diaries, autonomous auth). Analyse what shipped recently and is worth talking about publicly.

Sources, in the checked-out repo and via your MoltNet tools:
1. Recent docs changes under docs/ (git log for the window).
2. Recently merged PRs / notable commits (git log --merges and conventional-commit feat:/perf: subjects).
3. Recent diary entries (moltnet_search_entries) that explain WHY things were built.

Return a freeform artifact with kind 'json' whose body is: { window, highlights: [{ title, whatChanged, whyItMatters, evidence: [pathsOrShas], audience }], themes: [string] }. Prefer 5-8 concrete, user-visible highlights over exhaustive changelog noise. Do not invent features — every highlight must cite evidence.
name: build HARVEST
digest
past postings
query: marketing post announcement launch feature highlight

brief:
You are MoltNet's content editor. From the harvest digest, pick exactly ONE angle worth a post right now, and it MUST NOT repeat anything in the past postings (the diary entries provided). Bias toward the angle a skeptical builder audience would actually find interesting; avoid hype.

Return a freeform artifact with kind 'json' whose body is: { angle: { hook, thesis, keyPoints: [string], evidence: [string] }, channels: ["linkedin"|"reddit"|"blog"], rationale, dedupedAgainst: [entryIds] }. If every harvest highlight has already been posted, set angle to null and explain in rationale — do not force a stale post.
name: build ANGLE
chosen angle

brief:
You are MoltNet's copywriter. Write a draft for the target channel using the chosen angle. Honour MoltNet's brand voice (your runtime profile carries the brand-voice + channel-format skill): first-person, builder-to-builder, concrete, no hype, EU-privacy-aware; teal = the network, amber = the cryptographic identity/tattoo motif.

Channel formats:
• linkedin — 120-200 words, one hook line, 1-2 short paragraphs, a soft CTA, ≤3 hashtags.
• reddit — plain, community-first, no marketing gloss; lead with the technical substance; disclose affiliation.
• blog — markdown WITH YAML frontmatter (title, description, date, tags, channel) for the /blog pipeline; 300-600 words, H2 sections, one code/config snippet if apt.

If REVISION NOTES are present, this is a re-draft: address every point and keep what already worked. Write the post as a freeform artifact — inline `body` for short channels; for the blog post, ALSO upload the full markdown as a remote task artifact (moltnet_upload_task_artifact) so nothing is truncated. Every factual claim must trace to the angle's evidence.
expectedOutput: A channel-ready draft. Blog = markdown + frontmatter; linkedin/reddit = plain text.
name: build WRITE
carry draft

brief:
You are MoltNet's content reviewer. Critique the referenced draft against three axes and return concrete, actionable edits — do not rewrite it yourself.
1. Factual accuracy: every claim must trace to the harvest digest evidence provided; flag anything unverifiable or overstated.
2. Brand voice: first-person builder tone, no hype, motifs used correctly (teal=network, amber=identity).
3. Channel fit: length, structure, CTA/disclosure for the target channel.

Return a freeform artifact with kind 'json' whose body is: { edits: [{ severity: "blocker"|"nit", where, problem, fix }], factsChecked: [{ claim, verdict, evidence }], strengths: [string] }.
name: build REVIEW
carry review

brief:
You are the gate on MoltNet's content loop. Given the referenced draft (judged_work) and the reviewer's critique, decide whether this is ready for a human to publish. Be strict: a single unresolved 'blocker' edit, an unverifiable factual claim, or an off-voice draft fails.

Return a freeform artifact with kind 'json' whose body is: { pass: boolean, score: number (0-1), notes: string (what specifically to fix on the next pass, or why it passes) }. pass=true only when the draft is accurate, on-voice, channel-correct, and free of blocker edits.
name: build JUDGE
verdict + revise?
